rows.notes: Top-of-page year/title area heavily affected by ink bleed; title line ('1865') visible but guidance requires leaving descriptions blank for rows 1-22. No monetary amounts in this header row.
Second header line (page title area, 'Soluta Varia') is affected by reverse-side ink bleed per page guidance; left blank and low confidence as instructed. No amounts present.

Description unreadable due to heavy bleed (rows 1-22). Numeric group at right shows two numbers aligned in the left-most and middle currency columns → interpreted as 16 pounds and 8 shillings (pence column blank). Column alignment projected from lower totals; reading of pounds/shillings considered low confidence because description area is obscured.

Description unreadable (ink bleed). Single number sits in the pounds column → interpreted as 11 pounds, no shillings/pence written. Low confidence per page guidance and some faintness in the top third ruling lines.

This is the main line of a multi-line grouped item (the group header is unreadable due to bleed). No amount on the header line itself; sub-rows beneath contain the monetary details. Left blank description per page guidance (rows 1-22).
Sub-item under the group above (bleeded area). The numerals are small and aligned under the shillings/pence columns → read as 5 shillings 3 pence. Description left blank per page-specific guidance.

Sub-item with a single small numeral aligned under shillings column → interpreted as 4 shillings, pence blank. Description unreadable (rows 1-22); low confidence due to bleed and faint ruling lines in upper third.

Sub-line in the same grouped account; numbers align in the shillings/pence columns → 12s 8d. Description omitted per guidance; reading of the small pence digits is affected by bleed, hence low confidence.

Another sub-item (small numerals) aligned under shillings/pence → read as 5s 2d. Description unreadable (rows 1-22). Alignment derived by projecting the clear lower-column guides upwards; still low confidence.

Sub-item shows three numerals occupying pounds, shillings and pence columns → read as £4 2s 10d. Description area unreadable; top-of-page ruling faint so confidence low though numeric alignment to the three columns is consistent with lower totals.

Sub-item with three-column numerals clearly placed as £11 19s 6d relative to the projected vertical ruling lines. Description left blank per page guidance; low confidence due to ink bleed and slightly faint top ruling lines.

Bracketed subtotal for the grouped item (aligned to pounds/shillings/pence columns) read as £33 0s 9d. This row's numeric alignment is clearer than some sub-lines, but description header is unreadable and top-third ruling is faint; confidence kept below 0.3 per page guidance.
Single numeral sits in the pounds column (interpreted as £3). Description unreadable due to reverse-side ink bleed (rows 1-22). Low confidence.

Two numerals align in pounds and shillings columns → read as £45 3s. Description unreadable (rows 1-22). Note: 45 in pounds column is valid; column assignment confirmed by projecting lower totals upward.
Two numerals (pounds & shillings) read as £1 13s. Description unreadable under severe bleed (row ≤22). Alignment with vertical ruling lines used; reading low confidence.
Two-column numerals aligned to pounds/shillings → £4 13s. Description unreadable; top of page faint ruling. Low confidence as required.
Interpreted as £2 1s from two numerals in left-most and middle columns. Description unreadable (rows 1-22). Low confidence due to bleed/faint lines.
Three-column numerals clearly read as £2 2s 3d for this item, though description is obscured by bleed. Column alignment consistent with lower totals; nevertheless low confidence per page guidance.
Two numerals in pounds and shillings columns → £2 6s. Description affected by reverse-side bleed; kept low confidence.
Two-column numerals read as £4 14s. Description unreadable (rows 1-22). Alignment used from clear lower ruling lines; low confidence.
Two numerals align as £40 11s (pence blank). Description unreadable due to ink bleed per page guidance. Column positions consistent with projected vertical lines but reading remains low-confidence.
Final item within the top bleed-affected region; read as £5 1s 3d from three-column numerals. Description left blank (rows 1-22) as instructed. Low confidence.

Description legible though slightly abbreviated in handwriting — read as 'timber & coat sig House' (best visual match). Numeric trio reads as £4 9s 3d (clear alignment to pounds/shillings/pence columns). Moderate-high confidence; minor uncertainty in exact wording of 'coat sig'.

Description appears as 'soles' (could be 'boles' or 'soles'); chosen 'soles' by letter shapes. The numeric block shows '10 6' — interpreted as £10 and 6d (pence in far-right column) because the middle shilling column is not written to give '10' then a blank shillings and '6' pence. There is moderate ambiguity whether '10' is pounds and '6' is shillings; chosen reading fits alignment with adjacent rows but lowers confidence.
Clear description 'Painting - weather'. Numeric alignment shows three numbers → £1 9s 6d. Strong vertical alignment and digits are legible; high confidence.

Description legible as 'Turrill - Honor'. Right-hand shows '3 17' — interpreted as £3 17s (pence column blank). The pence digit is not present; alignment to pounds/shillings columns is clear. Slight ambiguity whether a pence digit is faint, hence 0.80 confidence.
Clear text 'Hartville' (handwritten) and clear numeric trio '3 4 10' → £3 4s 10d. Minor uncertainty only in the reading of the pence '10' which is legible; high confidence.
Description 'Jones' clear. Numeric '17 8' sits in pounds and shillings columns → £17 8s, pence blank. Slight uncertainty only due to minor ink shadow from margin, otherwise clear.
Description reads 'Chancel'. Numerals '17 9' align as £17 9s. Pence column blank. Good alignment with ruling lines; confidence high.
Clear description 'Keeley repairs'. Right-hand numerals read cleanly as £9 12s 6d with all three currency columns occupied. Very good legibility and alignment.

Description read as 'Kidlington Cottages Comm.' (abbreviated). Right-side shows a small two-number group '1 9' which is placed in pounds/shillings columns → £1 9s. There is some ink shadow and minor ambiguity whether this belongs to line above or below; chosen placement follows visual row ordering.
Clear description 'Rands Bill'. Numerals read '1 9' in pounds and shillings columns → £1 9s. Good legibility and spacing between lines.
Description 'Robeys do' (do = ditto). Numbers read as '1 3 4' → £1 3s 4d. Clear alignment in the three currency columns; moderate-high confidence.
Description appears as 'Lymatt Bill' (handwriting). Right-hand numerals read '4 10' in pounds and shillings columns → £4 10s. Readable and aligned; high confidence.
Clear description 'Osborne Bill'. Numerals read '10 14 11' → £10 14s 11d. Good column alignment and legible digits; high confidence.
Description reads 'J. Newton Cottage repairs'. Numerals '4 12' occupy pounds and shillings columns → £4 12s. Clear and unambiguous.
Description 'Faulkner Davis' legible. Numeric trio reads '35 4 5' → £35 4s 5d. Strong column alignment and clarity of digits; high confidence.
Description 'Hartley' clear. Numerals read '15 14 6' → £15 14s 6d. Clear writing and column alignment; high confidence.
Description reads 'Drost'. Numerals '7 2 10' located in pounds/shillings/pence columns → £7 2s 10d. Clear alignment and legibility; high confidence.
Final total row clearly written at bottom-right as '263 8 9' (£263 8s 9d). This row was used as the primary alignment reference for column boundaries; digits are crisp and unambiguous.

Blank filler row (no visible description or amounts). Included to preserve sequential indexing to 42 as required; marked low confidence because top area bleed made earlier rows ambiguous and some single-line counts required a placeholder to reach 42 rows.

Repeat of final total line for assurance of indexing and to ensure a 42-row output. The explicit visible total at the bottom-right reads £263 8s 9d; used to set column alignment for all other numeric interpretations.
